name: Hun Hunahpu
description: Hun Hunahpu, meaning "One Hunter," is a significant figure in Maya mythology, primarily known from the Popol Vuh, the sacred book of the K'iche' Maya. He is often depicted as a maize god and a central figure in the creation narrative. Hun Hunahpu's story is one of sacrifice, death, and rebirth, mirroring the cycle of maize cultivation. He, along with his brother Vucub Hunahpu (Seven Hunter), angered the lords of Xibalba, the Maya underworld, due to their noisy ball game playing. They were lured to Xibalba and subjected to trials and trickery. Hun Hunahpu's ultimate demise involved decapitation, and his head was hung in a calabash tree, which then bore fruit resembling his head. This act led to the miraculous birth of the Hero Twins, Hunahpu and Xbalanque, who avenged their father and uncle.
subtitle: One Hunter, Maize God

keyMyths.title: The Descent to Xibalba
keyMyths.description: Hun Hunahpu and Vucub Hunahpu were avid ballplayers whose games disturbed the lords of Xibalba. The lords, Hun Came and Vucub Came, invited them to the underworld under the pretense of a ballgame. However, the trials were designed to kill them. They failed the tests and were ultimately defeated, with Hun Hunahpu being sacrificed and his head placed in a calabash tree.

keyMyths.title: The Birth of the Hero Twins
keyMyths.description: Hun Hunahpu's head, placed in the calabash tree, impregnated Xquic, a daughter of one of the lords of Xibalba. She fled to the upperworld and gave birth to the Hero Twins, Hunahpu and Xbalanque. These twins inherited their father's skills and eventually avenged his death by outsmarting and defeating the lords of Xibalba in a series of trials and ballgames.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Humanity
keyMyths.description: After the Hero Twins' victory, they participated in the creation of humanity. The creation of humans from maize is a direct consequence of Hun Hunahpu's sacrifice and the subsequent actions of his sons. This act established maize as the staple food and a sacred element in Maya culture, linking Hun Hunahpu directly to the sustenance and spiritual well-being of the Maya people.
